hookSignals: POV hooks, relatable failure moments, that face when, emotional reaction to missing goals or breaking streaks
structure:
Line 1: Hook echo
Line 2: Bridge to Smash
Line 3: Concrete benefit
Line 4: CTA
guide: Line 1 mirrors the video hook in under 60 characters. Line 2 names Smash and states the core benefit in 3 to 5 words. Line 3 gives one concrete outcome the app enables (daily show up, streak repair, weekly check in) without invented stats. Line 4 matches the selected CTA. Total 125 to 220 characters. Max 2 emojis.
example:
POV: You just missed your goal 30 days in a row 😱
Smash makes showing up stupid simple.
Daily check ins + streak repair so you never miss twice.
Comment GOALS for free link ↓

hookSignals: Warm audience, direct ask, comment bait hooks, save this energy, viewers who already get goal tracking
structure:
Line 1: Strong CTA
Line 2: Why act now
Line 3: What they get
guide: Put the call to action in line 1 using the selected CTA wording. Line 2 explains the payoff in one sentence tied to the goal. Line 3 stacks one more benefit from the demo (milestones, journaling, weekly check ins). Under 200 characters. Best when the hook already sold the problem.
example:
Comment GOALS for the link ↓
You asked for a goal app that stops you from quitting.
Smash tracks streaks and calls you out when you skip.

hookSignals: Numbered hooks (3 reasons, 5 rules), listicle energy, educational tone, tips about goal failure or habit building
structure:
Line 1: Numbered hook
Body: 3 bullets
Last line: Tie to Smash
guide: Start with a number (3 reasons, 5 rules). Each bullet is one line, one insight about hitting goals. No fluff. Last line connects the value to Smash features shown in the video. 200 to 400 characters. Optimized for saves.
example:
3 reasons you keep failing your goals:
1. You track results not actions
2. You have zero accountability when motivation dips
3. You quit after missing 2 days
Smash fixes all 3 with daily check ins and streak repair.

hookSignals: Bold claim without revealing answer, watch till end, see what happens, mysterious transformation, one rule changed everything
structure:
Line 1: Open loop
Line 2: Hint not answer
Line 3: CTA
guide: Create tension with a bold claim about goal achievement. Do not reveal the answer in the caption — force them to watch. Reference a timestamp or watch till end when the hook supports it. End with CTA matching the selected CTA. Under 150 characters.
example:
This one rule made me stop quitting every goal 😱
See what happens at 0:06.
Comment GOALS if you want it.

hookSignals: Short meme format, me vs me, January person joke, under 10 word hooks, video carries most of the message
structure:
Line 1: Relatable joke or POV
Optional Line 2: Smash punchline
guide: Use a common meme format or POV. Keep line 1 under 50 characters. Must be instantly relatable to people who set goals. Optional second line ties to Smash in 5 to 8 words. Use when the demo video carries 90 percent of the value.
example:
Me vs the person I said I would be in January:
Smash keeps me honest.

hookSignals: Skepticism, another app?, too complicated, won't stick, free?, takes too long — doubt or pushback in the hook
structure:
Line 1: Yes, it's...
Line 2: Kill top objection
Line 3: Proof from demo
Line 4: CTA
guide: Line 1 starts with Yes, it's actually free or Yes, it takes 10 seconds. Line 2 addresses the main doubt about goal apps. Line 3 points to what the video shows (5 second check in, simple UI). Line 4 is CTA. No invented stats.
example:
Yes, Smash is actually free 😅
No fluff. No 50 features. Just daily check ins.
Watch it take 5 seconds to log your day.
Comment GOALS to try ↓

hookSignals: Transformation arc, used to vs now, quit by week 2 vs streak, before and after contrast in hook or goal
structure:
Line 1: Before
Line 2: After
Line 3: How (Smash)
Line 4: CTA
guide: Line 1 paints the painful before state tied to the goal. Line 2 shows the ideal after state. Line 3 says how Smash bridges them using features from the screen sequence. Line 4 is CTA. Keep each line short.
example:
Before: Quit every goal by week 2
After: Showing up daily on autopilot
How: Smash makes check ins non negotiable
Comment GOALS for free link ↓

hookSignals: Limited time, before paywall, start today, don't wait, last chance, while it's free
structure:
Line 1: Scarcity hook
Line 2: Cost of waiting
Line 3: CTA with urgency
guide: Line 1 uses time or access scarcity without fake countdowns or invented user counts. Line 2 states what happens if they delay starting their goal. Line 3 adds urgency to the CTA. Use sparingly — only when the hook already has urgency. No fabricated statistics.
example:
Still planning to start that goal next Monday?
Every day you wait is another day you don't show up.
Comment GOALS and start today ↓

hookSignals: Book quote, atomic habits, deep work, reading, planning session, journal, philosophy of consistency
structure:
Line 1: Passage excerpt
Line 2: Book + author
Line 3: Concept in plain words
Line 4: Tie to Smash
Line 5: CTA
guide: Use a passage 40 to 80 words from a real productivity, habits, or goals book (name the book and author). Line 3 breaks down the idea simply. Line 4 connects to how Smash helps (streaks, never miss twice, milestones). Line 5 is CTA. 300 to 450 characters. Use when the video shows reading, planning, or journaling.
example:
You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.
From Atomic Habits by James Clear
Consistency beats intensity — your system matters more than motivation.
Smash is the system that protects your streak so you never miss twice.
Comment GOALS to start yours ↓
